verses.2: You have planted them. Yes, they have taken root.
verses.4: How long will the land mourn,
verses.5: “If you have run with the footmen,
verses.6: For even your brothers, and the house of your father,
verses.8: My heritage has become to me as a lion in the forest.
verses.9: Is my heritage to me as a speckled bird of prey?
verses.10: Many shepherds have destroyed my vineyard.
verses.11: They have made it a desolation.
verses.12: Destroyers have come on all the bare heights in the wilderness;
verses.14: The LORD says, “Concerning all my evil neighbors, who touch the inheritance which I have caused my people Israel to inherit: Behold, I will pluck them up from off their land, and will pluck up the house of Judah from among them.
verses.15: It will happen that after I have plucked them up, I will return and have compassion on them. I will bring them again, every man to his heritage, and every man to his land.
verses.16: It will happen, if they will diligently learn the ways of my people, to swear by my name, ‘As the LORD lives;’ even as they taught my people to swear by Baal, then they will be built up in the middle of my people.
verses.17: But if they will not hear, then I will pluck up that nation, plucking up and destroying it,” says the LORD.
